chief_complaint: A 6-year-old boy presented with lethargy, vomiting, and bradycardia. A 10-year-old boy presented with acute abdominal pain.
history: Hepatitis A is usually a self-limiting infection in children, commonly presenting with jaundice. However, rare extra-hepatic manifestations may complicate the course, including cardiac and pancreatic involvement. Two paediatric cases of hepatitis A virus (HAV) infection with such atypical presentations are reported.
exam: Bilateral pleural effusion was noted in the 6-year-old boy. Imaging findings consistent with pancreatitis were found in the 10-year-old boy.
labs: Electrocardiogram revealed complete heart block progressing to Mobitz type I block in the 6-year-old boy. Elevated pancreatic enzymes were found in the 10-year-old boy.
correct_diagnosis: Hepatitis A virus (HAV) infection with pancreatitis
differentials: Hepatitis A virus (HAV) infection with pancreatitis
Pancreatitis due to other viral infections
Cardiac involvement due to HAV infection
correct_ranking: Hepatitis A virus (HAV) infection with pancreatitis
Pancreatitis due to other viral infections
Cardiac involvement due to HAV infection
